title: References to Public Awakening
summary: Drop #17 suggested that the public was beginning to recognize hidden corruption and institutional deception.
raw_text:
Why are people waking up?
Why are narratives failing?
What happens when people start asking questions?
Q

sources.label: Pew Research Center
sources.note: Research on public trust in institutions

lines.fact: Public trust in media and institutions declined significantly in parts of the U.S. population during the 2010s.
lines.theory: Q communities interpreted declining trust as evidence the public was discovering hidden truths.
lines.context: Distrust in institutions predates QAnon and reflects broader political polarization and digital media fragmentation.
lines.questions: How much of institutional distrust is driven by polarization versus verified misconduct?
lines.Deltas: 2020-2025: Trust in media and institutions remained a major political issue.
